title: This janitor died with almost $8 million #Shorts
desc: Ronald Read pumped gas for about 25 years, then worked as a JCPenney janitor for 17 more — yet when he died in 2014 he left a fortune of almost $8 million. He built it quietly, buying dividend-paying blue-chip stocks and holding them for decades as the dividends compounded. He gave $4.8 million to his town's hospital and $1.2 million to its library.
